title: Position monitor loop
acceptance_criteria: Constructor takes { clock: Clock, db: Pool, executor: PaperTradeExecutor, triggerConfig: TriggerConfig, staleThresholdMs?: number (default 30000) }

Subscribes to STREAM_STRADDLE Redis stream via streamConsume consumer group 'position-monitor'; on each snapshot: (1) load open positions via getOpenTrades() for today; (2) for each position: call updateTrailingStop() and persist the new lowestStraddleValueSeen to DB; (3) call evaluateTriggers(); (4) if shouldExit: call executor.closeTrade(); (5) ACK the Redis message AFTER all processing completes

CRITICAL — stale-data watchdog: independently of the Redis stream, register a VirtualClock.tick(watchdogIntervalMs=5000, callback) that fires every 5 seconds. Watchdog callback: checks clock.now() - lastTickTimestamp; if > staleThresholdMs, logs WARN 'Straddle feed stale for Ns'; ALSO evaluates time-based exits (EOD, EXIT_WINDOW) using the last known straddleValue for each open position and closes if triggered
CRITICAL — replay idempotency: before processing any snapshot (from stream or recovered pending), check if all open positions for today are already closed; if so, ACK immediately and skip processing
In-memory: maintains lastTickTimestamp (updated each time a real snapshot arrives) and lastKnownStraddleValue (updated each snapshot) for watchdog use
Exports start(): void and stop(): Promise<void> (graceful shutdown — stops consuming, waits for in-flight handler to complete)
XPENDING recovery: on start(), call recoverPending(STREAM_STRADDLE, 'position-monitor', consumerName) and replay any recovered messages through the same handler before starting new consumption
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
